name: align_to_one_changes_value
reason: Aligning to 1 should be a no-op since every integer is 1-aligned; a changed value would indicate a broken spec

name: nonzero_align_stays_nonzero
reason: A nonzero address aligned down can legitimately become zero (e.g., a=1 b=4096); if the spec claims otherwise it is too strong

name: aligned_additive_divisors
reason: Alignment to two sizes does not imply alignment to their sum (e.g., 12 is aligned to 4 and 6 but not to 10)

name: align_result_double_aligned
reason: Aligning down to b does not guarantee the result is also aligned to 2b (e.g., align_to_usize(5,4)==4 but 4 is not 8-aligned)
